reviews.context: Weekly self-improvement review covering the past 7 days of agent activity. Three patterns surfaced worth codifying into CLAUDE.md.

proposals.title: Pre-draft RFP responses on Wednesdays
proposals.pattern: Inbound RFP-style emails consistently land Thursday afternoons, but draft turnaround takes ~6 hours because brand context has to be re-gathered each time.
proposals.rule: Each Wednesday, pre-stage a discovery-call invite template and pull the latest case-study snippets so Thursday RFP replies can ship in under an hour.
proposals.why: Cuts response latency on the highest-value inbound from hours to minutes, which materially improves close rate on time-sensitive RFPs.

proposals.title: Lead social posts with a concrete outcome
proposals.pattern: Posts opening with a specific client result outperform abstract framework posts ~3x on engagement, but the social drafter defaults to framework-first openings.
proposals.rule: When drafting LinkedIn or X content, open with a concrete, quantified client outcome before introducing any framework or principle.
proposals.why: Engagement is the leading indicator for newsletter list growth, which is a Q2 goal currently at risk.

proposals.title: Confirm scheduling preference before proposing slots
proposals.pattern: The scheduler proposed slots that conflicted with Jane's Tuesday/Thursday deep-work blocks twice this week, requiring manual correction.
proposals.rule: Always query the calendar-preference memories (working-hours, deep-work) before proposing any external meeting slot.
proposals.why: Prevents back-and-forth and protects protected focus time, reducing manual intervention.

reviews.context: Weekly self-improvement review. Two proposals around cost discipline and draft quality.

proposals.title: Cap research tasks at a token budget
proposals.pattern: A single research task consumed 3x the typical token budget chasing tangential sources without adding value to the summary.
proposals.rule: For research tasks, set a soft token ceiling and stop gathering once the core question is answered with two corroborating sources.
proposals.why: Keeps per-task cost predictable and protects the daily budget ceiling.

proposals.title: Run a banned-phrase pass on every client draft
proposals.pattern: Two drafts this week included hype phrases the brand voice forbids, caught only at human review.
proposals.rule: Before marking any client-facing draft in_review, run a banned-phrase check and rewrite any flagged lines.
proposals.why: Reduces review burden and keeps every draft on-voice without manual cleanup.
